beforeAfter.before: Outdated templates, slow Mapbox queries, and lack of visual flow led to massive client bounces.
beforeAfter.after: Bespoke high-contrast layout, 1.2s load speed, cinematic property viewports, and automated inquiries.

timeline.label: Research
timeline.desc: Stakeholder mapping, competitor reviews, and layout exploration.

timeline.label: Design System
timeline.desc: Creation of black-bordered high-contrast brutalist design assets.

timeline.label: Development
timeline.desc: React/Next.js frontend build, Mapbox tile integrations, and Sanity CMS setup.

sections.title: The Challenge
sections.content: Meridian Properties needed a digital presence that matched the caliber of their $10M+ listings. Their existing site was template-based, slow, and failed to convey the exclusivity of their brand. Prospective buyers were bouncing before engaging with listings.

sections.title: Our Approach
sections.content: We conducted extensive user research with high-net-worth individuals to understand their browsing patterns. The result: an immersive, map-first experience with cinematic property presentations, virtual walkthroughs, and a concierge-style inquiry flow that respects their time.

sections.title: The Outcome
sections.content: A bespoke platform featuring interactive 3D floor plans, neighborhood intelligence overlays on Mapbox, and a private listing portal. Every transition is carefully choreographed — parallax scrolling, smooth page transitions, and micro-interactions that feel considered, never gratuitous.

beforeAfter.before: Bloated app dashboards, slow Plaid syncing feeds, and cluttered stock lists that confused non-tech users.
beforeAfter.after: Sleek biometric-login mobile client, sub-200ms D3 charts, and consolidated multicurrency wallet listings.

timeline.label: Kickoff
timeline.desc: Defining D3 chart metrics and Plaid API sandbox keys.

timeline.label: Engine Build
timeline.desc: React Native coding, Plaid routing, and Postgres optimization.

sections.title: The Challenge
sections.content: Vault Finance wanted to disrupt the portfolio tracking space dominated by bloated, ad-heavy incumbents. They needed a mobile-first experience that unifies brokerage accounts, crypto wallets, and alternative assets in one clean interface.

sections.title: Our Approach
sections.content: We designed a data-dense yet readable interface using custom D3.js visualizations. Every chart, every number, every interaction was stress-tested with real portfolio data across diverse asset classes. The design system scales from a single stock to a multi-million dollar portfolio.

sections.title: The Outcome
sections.content: A cross-platform mobile app with real-time portfolio syncing via Plaid, custom charting engine, AI-powered insights, and biometric security. The app handles 50K+ concurrent connections with sub-200ms response times.

beforeAfter.before: MVP dashboard dashboards had excessive load water-falls, complex forms, and poor navigation menus.
beforeAfter.after: Insight-first analytical tabs, Stripe billing modules, and serverless database scaling.

timeline.label: Scoping
timeline.desc: Auditing MVP features and mapping Stripe annual plans.

timeline.label: UI Design
timeline.desc: Re-designing tabular charts for quick user scanning.

timeline.label: Platform Build
timeline.desc: Next.js pages, Recharts widgets, Supabase schema models.

sections.title: The Challenge
sections.content: Prism Analytics had a powerful data engine but their MVP interface was confusing non-technical users. They were losing trials at the dashboard stage — users couldn't find their metrics without a guided tour.

sections.title: Our Approach
sections.content: We rebuilt the entire dashboard from first principles. User interviews revealed that operators want answers, not charts. So we designed an insight-first interface: key metrics surfaced automatically, anomalies highlighted, and drill-down available on demand.

sections.title: The Outcome
sections.content: A complete SaaS platform with role-based dashboards, automated reporting, Stripe-integrated billing, and a white-label option. The rebuild cut onboarding time by 74% and became the primary driver of word-of-mouth growth.

beforeAfter.before: Manual bookings, heavy spreadsheet lists, constant patient no-shows, and high desk hours.
beforeAfter.after: Fully automated SMS reminder pipelines, self-service portals, and direct clinic calendar scheduling.

timeline.label: Clinic Audits
timeline.desc: Shadowing clinic receptionists to map scheduling friction.

timeline.label: HIPAA Design
timeline.desc: Securing data flows and mock patient layouts.

timeline.label: Engineering
timeline.desc: React frontend, AWS Lambda workers, Twilio phone triggers.

sections.title: The Challenge
sections.content: Healthcare providers were losing revenue to no-shows and double-bookings. Existing scheduling tools were built for general purpose — not for the complexity of multi-provider, multi-location medical practices with insurance verification requirements.

sections.title: Our Approach
sections.content: We embedded ourselves in three clinics for two weeks, observing front-desk workflows. The result: a scheduling system that thinks like a medical receptionist — handling insurance pre-auth, provider availability rules, and patient preferences simultaneously.

sections.title: The Outcome
sections.content: An intelligent scheduling platform with SMS/email reminders via Twilio, insurance pre-verification, waitlist management, and telehealth integration. The system auto-fills cancelled slots from the waitlist, recovering an average of $12K/month per clinic in otherwise-lost revenue.
